task_completion.description: Open the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset in NASA Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application window is open, showing the 'air.mon.ltm.nc' dataset in the sources/datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Generate a plot for January air temperature with locked colorbar limits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying the 'air' variable for January (time index 1 or similar). The colorbar scale must be explicitly locked to a minimum of 290 K and a maximum of 310 K (visible on the colorbar itself or in the 'Scale' tab settings).

task_completion.description: Save the January plot as australia_jan_heat.png in the RailRisk directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing 'australia_jan_heat.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/RailRisk/' directory.

task_completion.description: Generate a plot for July air temperature with locked colorbar limits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying the 'air' variable for July (time index 7 or similar). The colorbar scale must be explicitly locked to a minimum of 270 K and a maximum of 290 K.

task_completion.description: Save the July plot as australia_jul_cold.png in the RailRisk directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing 'australia_jul_cold.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/RailRisk/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the thermal buckling report with extracted temperatures and computed amplitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'thermal_buckling_report.txt' in '~/Documents/RailRisk/'. The file must contain extracted temperatures for January and July, and a computed seasonal thermal amplitude.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply graphical interface being interacted with to create the plots, rather than terminal commands running a Python script to bypass the tool.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or generated the text report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report should be visible in a text editor (like nano, gedit, or VS Code) or created via terminal echo/cat commands, not just copied from a pre-existing hidden file.

integrity.description: The extracted temperatures and amplitude in the report are genuine calculations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the report should roughly match the expected ranges (Jan: ~304-307 K, Jul: ~284-287 K, Amplitude: ~17-23 K) and the amplitude must be the actual mathematical difference between the stated Jan and Jul temperatures.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is air.mon.ltm.nc. The expected outputs in ~/Documents/RailRisk/ are australia_jan_heat.png, australia_jul_cold.png, and thermal_buckling_report.txt. The mandated colorbar scale limits for Panoply are 290-310 K for January and 270-290 K for July. Valid extracted temperatures for the central Australian interior should be approximately 304.8 - 306.8 K for January and 284.2 - 286.9 K for July, yielding a thermal amplitude of approximately 17.9 - 22.5 K.
